questions.q: In which year did Qutb-ud-din Aibak become the first Sultan of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak became the first independent Sultan of Delhi in 1206 CE after the death of Muhammad of Ghor.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak founded which dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate?
questions.explanation: He founded the Mamluk or Slave dynasty, the first of the five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Before becoming Sultan, what had been Qutb-ud-din Aibak's status?
questions.explanation: Aibak was originally a slave, rising to become a trusted general before founding his own dynasty as Sultan.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak began construction of which famous monument in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Aibak started the construction of the Qutb Minar around 1199 CE, a tower later completed by Iltutmish.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak was a slave general of which ruler?
questions.explanation: Aibak served as a slave general under Muhammad of Ghor, who placed him in charge of Indian conquests.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak is regarded as the founder of which medieval Indian state?
questions.explanation: As the first Sultan of Delhi in 1206, Aibak is considered the founder of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: In which century did Qutb-ud-din Aibak rule as Sultan of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Aibak ruled as Sultan from 1206 until his death in 1210, in the early 13th century CE.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar, begun by Aibak, is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Qutb Minar stands in Delhi, in the complex named after Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak's dynasty is also commonly called the:
questions.explanation: Because Aibak and several successors had been slaves, his line is often called the Slave dynasty.

questions.q: Which Sultanate did Aibak's founding inaugurate that would last until 1526?
questions.explanation: Aibak's accession in 1206 began the Delhi Sultanate, which endured through five dynasties until 1526.

questions.q: From which city did Qutb-ud-din Aibak chiefly rule his early domain?
questions.explanation: Aibak primarily based himself at Lahore during his short reign, though Delhi was the symbolic seat of his power.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak became independent ruler after the death of Muhammad of Ghor in which year?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor was assassinated in 1206, after which Aibak assumed independent authority in northern India.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak earned a reputation for generosity that gave him which epithet?
questions.explanation: Aibak was nicknamed Lakh-baksh, meaning giver of hundreds of thousands, for his lavish charity and gifts.

questions.q: The mosque adjoining the Qutb Minar, built under Aibak, is known as the:
questions.explanation: Aibak built the Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque, among the earliest mosques in India, beside the rising Qutb Minar.

questions.q: How did Qutb-ud-din Aibak die in 1210 CE?
options: A fall from his horse while playing chaugan (polo)
questions.explanation: Aibak died at Lahore in 1210 after falling from his horse during a game of chaugan, a form of polo.

questions.q: Who briefly succeeded Qutb-ud-din Aibak before being displaced?
questions.explanation: Aram Shah succeeded Aibak briefly in 1210 but was soon set aside in favour of the more capable Iltutmish.

questions.q: Which capable general and son-in-law eventually became the third Sultan after Aibak?
questions.explanation: Iltutmish, Aibak's slave and son-in-law, became the third Sultan and consolidated the Sultanate.

questions.q: Qutb-ud-din Aibak was of which ethnic origin?
questions.explanation: Aibak was a Turkic slave from Central Asia, part of the military slave (mamluk) tradition of the Ghurids.

questions.q: Before 1206, in whose name did Aibak govern the conquered territories in India?
questions.explanation: As a deputy, Aibak administered the Indian conquests on behalf of his master Muhammad of Ghor until the latter's death.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Qutb-ud-din Aibak reign as Sultan of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Aibak reigned only about four years, from 1206 to his death in 1210, leaving consolidation to his successors.

questions.q: The Qutb Minar and Quwwat-ul-Islam Mosque are said to incorporate materials from earlier demolished structures of which faith near the site?
questions.explanation: The early Qutb complex reused stonework from local Hindu and Jain temples, visible in its pillars and carvings.

questions.q: Aibak's adopted laqab (honorific) Qutb-ud-din carries which meaning?
options: Axis or pole of the faith
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din translates roughly as 'axis (or pole) of the faith', a common honorific title of the era.

questions.q: In which present-day country is Qutb-ud-din Aibak buried?
questions.explanation: Aibak died at Lahore and his tomb is located there, in present-day Pakistan.

questions.q: Aibak rose under the Ghurids, who supplanted which earlier Turkic power in the region of Ghazni?
questions.explanation: The Ghurids, Aibak's overlords, displaced the earlier Ghaznavid dynasty in Afghanistan and the northwest.

questions.q: Aibak's contemporary Bakhtiyar Khalji is famous for conquering which eastern region?
questions.explanation: Ikhtiyar-ud-din Bakhtiyar Khalji, active under the Ghurid expansion, overran Bengal and Bihar in this period.

questions.q: Aibak's marriage alliances helped tie him to which other prominent Ghurid slave general?
questions.explanation: Aibak was linked by marriage to fellow Ghurid commanders Yildiz and Qabacha, rivals in the post-Ghurid succession.

questions.q: Aibak's authority as Sultan was somewhat constrained because he never received which formal recognition?
options: A letter of investiture (manshur) from the Caliph
questions.explanation: Unlike Iltutmish later, Aibak did not obtain formal investiture from the Abbasid Caliph, leaving his sovereignty less consolidated.

questions.q: The dynasty Aibak founded is technically better termed 'Mamluk' rather than a hereditary house because:
options: It ruled jointly with the Caliph
Its rulers were elected by the army
Several of its sultans were of slave origin rather than blood relations
questions.explanation: The Mamluk label reflects that power passed among slave-origin commanders, not strictly from father to son.

questions.q: Aibak first entered service when, as a youth, he was purchased by a qazi in which city?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that the young Aibak was bought by a qazi of Nishapur, who had him educated before he entered Ghurid service.

questions.q: Aibak's founding of independent rule is seen as the start of a new era partly because he ruled without continued allegiance to which centre?
questions.explanation: After Muhammad of Ghor's death, Aibak governed independently of the fragmenting Ghurid centre, beginning indigenous Sultanate rule.
